mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in less than 50% of rate-cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of instances where unemployment rises above the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business confidence, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in more than 55% of triggered cases or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a tightening cycle reflects resilient economic output that offsets higher borrowing costs, yielding positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Industrial production gains fail to predict positive 5-day returns when federal funds rate increases exceed 25 basis points per meeting.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth above trend levels signals expanding corporate earnings capacity, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window or GDP growth shows no predictive power for quarterly equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally in more than 65% of months where inflation exceeds the threshold, or the signal loses predictive power after controlling for rate expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment indicates a strengthening labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate profits, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative forward returns in more than 50% of observations or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Declining retail sales alongside rising inflation signals weakening demand amid price pressures, forcing equity repricing and producing negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The dual signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns in more than 60% of cases or performs no better than a random baseline in walk-forward testing.
